steps.text: Identify the sampling method. Viewers of a cable news show voluntarily visited the show's website and chose to respond. This is a voluntary response (self-selected) sample, not a random sample.

For a poll to represent the US population, respondents must be randomly selected. Here, only motivated show viewers responded — they share demographics and viewpoints not representative of the broader public.

Evaluate each choice:
  • Choice A: The percentages sum to 98%, meaning 2% may have skipped or chosen another option. A gap this small does not invalidate conclusions; representativeness is the real issue.
  • Choice B: Respondents self-selected from the show's audience; they were not a random sample of the US population. This directly explains why results are unlikely to represent national sentiments. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • Choice C: There is no rule requiring a valid poll to show a 50/50 split. An unequal split is perfectly valid in a properly conducted random sample.
  • Choice D: The amount of time given to respond does not address the core problem of non-random, self-selected participation.
